goal: Pivot from failed full-surface directional prediction to base-conditional trade-quality overlays — entry veto, early-cut, and hold-extension — that improve the immutable base without retuning its logic.
global_constraints: Base snapshot active-signal-v1@1 is immutable — never modify its signals, thresholds, hours, days, or execution logic
No full-surface directional classification or regression on future PnL (v4–v7 routes terminally closed)
All v8 work operates on trade-level surface of already-executed base trades, not on all anchor bars
Feature selection, label selection, and threshold selection only on selection OOS — never on lockbox
Overlay labels must be frozen before any model training begins
Do not mix entry-veto and exit-extension objectives in a single label
Purge gap between train / selection-OOS / lockbox windows must be ≥ max holding horizon (minimum 24h)
Every improvement claim requires matched lockbox OOS comparison against immutable base on same trade window with same notionals

stages.title: Closure-audit v7 and v8 invariant fixation
stages.objective: Formally record the terminal failure of v7 full-surface directional classification, open the v8 research project, and fix all immutable references and map axes.
actions: Open a new research_project for v8
Record v7 terminal verdict via research_record: full-surface classification route terminally failed, reasons for closure, rationale for v8 pivot
Fix immutable reference: snapshot_id=active-signal-v1, version=1, symbol=BTCUSDT, anchor=1h, execution=5m
Create research_map axes: overlay_route, side, feature_family, label_family, model_stage, final_decision
Create open-items register: trade_surface_atlas, veto_label_contract, veto_long, veto_short, earlycut_route_lock, extension_route_lock, final_overlay_verdict
success_criteria: v8 research project created and active
v7 terminal verdict formally recorded in research memory
Immutable base reference locked with all five fields
Research map initialised with all six axes
Open-items register created with all seven items

stages.title: Build trade-surface atlas of immutable base
stages.objective: Transition from anchor-level to trade-level thinking by extracting every executed base trade with full context and building analytical cuts across the trade surface.
actions: Collect all trades of the immutable base over the full available period
For each trade record: side, entry time, exit time, realized PnL, MFE, MAE, time-in-trade, exit reason, weak-window membership, context at entry, path in first 1h/3h/6h
Build cuts: long vs short, winners vs losers, stop-loss vs time-limit vs take-profit, weak quarters, weakest pockets
Separately identify: worst-decile trades, fragile trades, underheld winners
success_criteria: Complete trade surface extracted covering full data window
All per-trade fields populated including MFE, MAE, path shape, exit reason
Analytical cuts produced for long/short, winner/loser, exit-type, and weak windows
Worst-decile, fragile, and underheld-winner clusters explicitly identified

stages.title: Freeze overlay label contracts
stages.objective: Define and freeze all overlay labels (veto, early-cut, extension) before any model training, including ambiguity policy and material improvement thresholds.
actions: Define veto labels: bad_trade_balanced, bad_trade_conservative for each side
Define early-cut labels: cut_now_balanced, cut_now_conservative
Define extension labels: extend_hold_balanced, extend_hold_conservative
Fix ambiguity policy for near-zero trades, mixed trades, and what counts as material improvement
success_criteria: All six label definitions frozen with explicit positive-class criteria
Ambiguity policy documented for near-zero and mixed trades
Material improvement threshold defined quantitatively
Labels recorded in research memory before any training

stages.title: Feature contract for trade-quality overlays
stages.objective: Author, validate, publish, and materialize all new trade-context feature families (A–D) on the base trade surface with warmup/coverage/causality checks.
actions: Priority 1 — Entry-context features for veto route: cf_entry_stretch_6h, cf_entry_range_position_12h, cf_entry_exhaustion_score, cf_signal_freshness, cf_confluence_density, cf_trade_eth_btc_confirmation, cf_trade_breadth_confirmation, cf_trade_event_headwind
Priority 2 — Post-entry features for early-cut: cf_first_1h_followthrough, cf_first_3h_adverse_excursion, cf_post_entry_instability, cf_early_failure_signature
Priority 3 — Exit-time features for extension: cf_mfe_before_time_limit, cf_trend_persistence_at_time_limit, cf_exit_context_strength, cf_reversal_risk_at_time_limit, cf_extension_quality_score
For each feature: write contract, validate, publish, materialize, verify warmup/coverage/causality
success_criteria: All Priority 1 features validated, published, and materialized with acceptable warmup and coverage
Priority 2 and 3 features authored and validated (materialization may follow gate)
No feature leaks future information relative to trade entry timestamp
Feature catalog updated with all new custom features

stages.title: Dataset split and modeling surfaces for overlays
stages.objective: Establish trade-level temporal OOS splits (train / selection-OOS / lockbox) by entry timestamp and assemble per-side modeling surfaces with frozen feature sets.
actions: Split trades by entry timestamp into train_trades, selection_oos_trades, lockbox_oos_trades
Assemble surfaces: all_base_trades, long_base_trades, short_base_trades, weak_window_trades if needed
Enforce purge gap ≥ 24h between windows
Lock feature set and threshold selection to selection OOS only
success_criteria: Temporal split completed with no overlap and purge gap ≥ 24h
All modeling surfaces materialized with correct features and frozen labels
Feature set selection locked to selection OOS
Dataset contract recorded and immutable

stages.title: Veto-route plausibility on selection OOS
stages.objective: Train CatBoost veto classifiers per side and label family, then evaluate on selection OOS whether worst base trades can be reliably identified.
actions: Train CatBoost-veto-short-balanced and CatBoost-veto-short-conservative on short trade surface
Train CatBoost-veto-long-balanced and CatBoost-veto-long-conservative on long trade surface
Evaluate on selection OOS: ROC AUC, PR AUC, worst-decile lift, precision in veto bucket, economic effect of removing top-risk trades
success_criteria: At least one veto model achieves ROC AUC meaningfully above 0.50 on selection OOS
Worst-decile lift is positive — model ranks bad trades higher
Economic simulation of vetoing top-risk trades shows non-trivial PnL/PF improvement on selection OOS
Results recorded with full metrics in research memory

stages.title: Lockbox test for veto overlays
stages.objective: Apply the best veto models on the untouched lockbox window and measure whether the overlay materially improves the immutable base on strictly out-of-sample trades.
actions: Freeze thresholds from best veto models
Run immutable base on lockbox trades as reference
Run base + veto overlay on same lockbox trades
Compute: delta PnL, delta PF, delta DD, trades removed, PnL of removed trades, effect by weak windows
success_criteria: Lockbox comparison is matched — same trades, same notionals, same window
Material improvement delta quantified across PnL, PF, DD
Effect on weak windows separately assessed
Veto route formally promoted, watchlisted, or rejected

stages.title: Early-cut route — conditional on veto signal
stages.objective: Investigate post-entry early-cut overlays only if the veto route demonstrated at least weak meaningful signal, to determine if failing trades can be rescued mid-flight.
actions: Train CatBoost-earlycut-long and CatBoost-earlycut-short on respective surfaces
Evaluate on selection OOS: improvement vs baseline exit on same trades, net delta after fees, DD improvement, false-cut rate on future winners
success_criteria: Early-cut models trained with frozen labels on correct trade surfaces
Selection OOS shows improvement over baseline exit with positive net delta after fees
Model cuts real losers more often than future winners
stages.gate_hint: Early-cut route opens only if veto models showed at least weak meaningful signal on selection OOS and analysis indicates bad trades are recognizable in post-entry phase

stages.title: Hold-extension route — conditional on underheld winners
stages.objective: Test whether a rare class of underheld winning trades can be identified and held longer for incremental PnL, only if the trade-surface atlas and selection diagnostics support it.
actions: Train CatBoost-extend-long and CatBoost-extend-short on respective surfaces
Evaluate on selection OOS: incremental PnL on extension subset, extension hit rate, DD impact
success_criteria: Extension models trained with frozen labels
Selection OOS shows positive incremental PnL on extension subset
Extension does not materially worsen drawdown
Hit rate of extensions is meaningfully above random
stages.gate_hint: Extension route opens only if trade-surface atlas showed underheld winner cluster and early-cut or selection diagnostics give positive evidence that extension has a chance

stages.title: Ownership, cannibalization analysis, and final overlay verdict
stages.objective: Compute ownership and cannibalization for every surviving overlay route, render a final honest verdict (success / partial / failed), and close v8 with a complete report.
actions: For each surviving overlay route compute: ownership attribution, bad trades removed / DD saved, overlap with weak-window clusters, real vs cosmetic improvement assessment
Render final verdict per route: promote, watchlist, or kill
Record overall v8 outcome: overlay success (A), partial (B), or failed (C)
Write complete v8 closure report with final decision table
success_criteria: Ownership analysis completed for every surviving overlay
Cannibalization risk assessed — no overlay worsens net outcome
Final verdict rendered with clear decision per route
v8 closure report written with all result fields populated
Next direction documented based on outcome

warnings: Plan is in Russian; stage titles and objectives are translated to English in the semantic draft while preserving original intent
Stages 8 and 9 are gated on positive evidence from prior stages — the plan may terminate earlier if veto route shows no signal
